data.title: General Catalyst Global Resilience Merger Corp. Files for IPO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

General Catalyst Global Resilience Merger Corp. has filed for an initial public offering.

The special purpose acquisition company, an affiliate of venture capital firm General Catalyst, said it will sell 35 million units at $10 apiece, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday. Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-fourth of one redeemable warrant.

The company has also granted underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 5.3 million units to cover over-allotments. Citigroup is listed as the offering's underwriter.

The company additionally said that a sponsor has committed to purchase 800,000 private placement units at $10 per unit for an aggregate price of $8 million, or about $9.1 million if the underwriters' over-allotment option is exercised in full.

The private placement is expected to close simultaneously with the closing of the IPO.

The company said it will receive gross proceeds of $358 million from the offering, or about $411.6 million if the over-allotment option is exercised. Both figures include proceeds from the private placement.

The Cayman Islands blank-check company plans to acquire businesses in the physical artificial intelligence, manufacturing, industrial operations and alternative energy industries, among others.

The company intends to apply to have its units listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker GCGRU. It expects the Class A ordinary shares and warrants comprising the securities will begin separate trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols GCGR and GCGRW, respectively.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: McDonald's to Add Energy Drinks, Crafted Sodas to Menus — WSJ
data.description:
By Heather Haddon

The world's biggest burger chain is entering the world of energy drinks and specialty sodas.

McDonald's is set to offer a Red Bull Dragonberry Energizer as part of a broader overhaul of its menu of cold drinks at its U.S. restaurants later this year, according to people familiar with the matter and company documents viewed by The Wall Street Journal.

The new beverages, slated to roll out next month, include a Dirty Dr Pepper and Mango Pineapple Refresher, according to the documents. The company's slate of energy drinks are expected to be sold starting in August.

The fast-food company has spent years plotting its beverage expansion, including tests of Sour Cherry Energy Burst and Blackberry Mint Green Tea drinks at the company's short-lived concept called CosMc's.

McDonald's plans to sell its new beverages at prices below those of competitors such as Starbucks, Dutch Bros, Sonic and other chains, the documents show. Shares of coffee chains Dutch Bros and Black Rock Coffee Bar, which both do big business in iced beverages and energy drinks, declined on Monday.

McDonald's expects the new drinks to deliver high profit margins for franchisees, which operate the bulk of its restaurants. Franchisees have invested thousands of dollars in new equipment per restaurant to mix the drinks, hoping to ring up more sales while not slowing service, the people said. The company said it involved operators in determining how to best prepare the beverages.

Coca-Cola, McDonald's main beverage partner, is a supplier for some but not all of the new drinks. Coke's Sprite and Hi-C are included in two of the drinks making a debut in May, while Keurig Dr Pepper-owned Dr Pepper is featured in a third, the documents show.

An energy drink launching in August includes Red Bull, and was also part of last year's McDonald's test.

Sales of energy drinks and crafted sodas are booming as Americans look beyond coffee and tea to get their caffeine fix and treat themselves. Restaurants reported 9% more energy-drink orders over the 12 months ended in February compared with the same period a year earlier, while orders for coffee and tea fell, according to market-research firm Circana.

Upstart beverage chains like Swig have quickly grown in recent years in response to demand for so-called dirty sodas and fruity refreshers. Dutch Bros's energy drinks have helped it to become the third-biggest U.S. coffee chain.

Big restaurant operators are racing to catch up. Taco Bell added Mountain Dew Baja Blast dirty sodas and Tropicana Original dirty lemonades to its permanent menu in March, part of the chain's effort to build a $5 billion beverage business by 2030.

Starbucks this month launched its own caffeinated Refreshers drinks, along with the option to add energy shots to its existing line of fruity drinks.

McDonald's has said it wants a larger piece of the more than $100 billion global beverage market. In 2023, McDonald's introduced its CosMc's offshoot to see how customers reacted to novelty drinks. After the company shuttered CosMc's last year, it began testing new beverages in around 500 restaurants in September.

The tests showed McDonald's needed a diverse drinks lineup to break through the crowded beverage market, said Charlie Newberger, the chain's global head of beverages. The company also learned what customers didn't want, such as the lattes flavored with matcha or turmeric sold at CosMc's.

"They are looking for that little treat, that companion to the day," Newberger told the Journal last year. "Those play to our strengths."

Write to Heather Haddon at heather.haddon@wsj.com

Watch: McDonald's CEO Discusses Menu Strategy and Other Concerns

data.title: Spot basis bids flat as crop conditions worsen
data.description:
Basis bids for hard red winter (HRW) wheat were unchanged in the U.S. Plains states on Monday as farmers and market players assessed crop conditions and weather.

  • Weekend rains missed some of the driest areas of the U.S. Plains, an analyst note said.

  • Dryness will continue to cause stress for wheat in the central and western Plains, forecaster Vaisala said.

  • After trading ended, the U.S. Department of Agriculture said 34% of the U.S. winter wheat crop was in good or excellent condition as of Sunday. That was down from 35% a week earlier and 47% a year ago.

  • K.C. May contract (KWK26) ended up 12-1/2 cents at $6.03-1/4 a bushel and set its highest price since April 3.

data.title: Bloom Energy to Supply Up to 2.8GW of Fuel Cell Power for Oracle in Expanded Partnership
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Bloom Energy will supply up to 2.8 gigawatts of fuel cell energy systems for Oracle in an expanded partnership between the two companies.

Oracle has contracted an initial 1.2 gigawatts of capacity from Bloom, with deployment expected to continue into next year, the companies said Monday.

Bloom Energy shares jumped 9.7% to $194.13 in after-hours trading Monday. The stock closed up 6% at $176.67, having more than doubled in value this year.

The companies said the expanded partnership will support the buildout of Oracle's artificial-intelligence and cloud-computing infrastructure, adding that it reflects a shift toward distributed, onsite energy generation for digital infrastructure.

"By rapidly deploying Bloom's reliable, efficient fuel cell energy, we are quickly meeting the demands of our customers across the United States," Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Executive Vice President Mahesh Thiagarajan said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Peak oil price likely to come 'in next few weeks,' US Energy secretary says
data.description:
Oil prices are likely to hit their peak "in the next few weeks" once ship traffic resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Monday.

Prices are expected to continue rising until "meaningful" ship traffic resumes through the strait, Wright told the Semafor World Economy Forum in Washington, despite previous comments he made that oil prices would likely come down soon.

"We're going to see energy prices high - and maybe even rising - until we get meaningful ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz," Wright said. "That'll probably hit the peak oil price at that time. That's probably sometime in the next few weeks."

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the price of oil and gasoline may remain high until November's midterm elections, a rare admission of the potential political fallout from his decision to attack Iran six weeks ago.

data.title: SANA Stock Rallies 24% After Hours — How Much Is Mayo Clinic Investing In The Company?
data.description:
Key points:
  • Sana expects to seek the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s clearance to start clinical trials and begin early stage trials for it as early as this year. 
  • Sana on Monday said that Mayo purchased 7.5 million shares of the company’s common stock at a price of $3.33 per share for gross proceeds of about $25 million under the deal.
  • The purchase price under the agreement implies a potential upside of about 4% from the stock's closing price on Monday.

Shares of Sana Biotechnology, Inc. (SANA) jumped 24% after-hours on Monday after the company announced a strategic collaboration with Mayo Clinic that involves an equity investment by the latter.

The agreement is aimed at advancing the development of its investigational cell therapy SC451 for type 1 diabetes. The company expects to seek the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s clearance to start clinical trials and begin early stage trials for it as early as this year.

Sana’s SC451 is designed to support long-term glucose control without the need for ongoing insulin therapy in type 1 diabetes patients. The agreement with Mayo is aimed at accelerating development, validation, and standardization of protocols and processes for SC451, and supporting safe, scalable, and consistent delivery across diverse clinical environments.

Equity Investment

Sana on Monday said that Mayo purchased 7.5 million shares of the company’s common stock at a price of $3.33 per share for gross proceeds of about $25 million under the deal. It also noted that Mayo would have the option to make an additional equity investment of $25 million as part of the collaboration.

The closing of the sale of the initial $25 million worth of shares is expected to occur on or about April 15, Sana said.

The purchase price under the agreement implies a potential upside of about 4% from the stock's closing price on Monday and nearly 8% from the closing price on Friday.

Wall Street Take

According to data from Koyfin, eight of the nine analysts covering SANA rate it ‘Buy’ or higher, while one rates it ‘Hold.’ The 12-month average price target on the stock is $8.43, representing a potential upside of about 163% from the stock’s last closing price.

How Did Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around SANA stock rose from ‘neutral’ to ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume increased from ‘low’ to ‘normal’ levels.

SANA stock has gained 77% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Chevron Agrees to Asset Swap With PdVSA
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Chevron said its subsidiaries with interests in Venezuela agreed to an asset swap with Petroleos de Venezuela, or PdVSA, which will help consolidate the parties' focus on strategic assets.

The deal will see Chevron receive an additional 13.21% working interest in the Petroindependencia joint venture, bringing the company's total stake to 49%. Another joint venture, Petropiar, which is 30% owned by a Chevron subsidiary, will receive rights to develop an area located in Venezuela's Orinoco Oil Belt.

Chevron said its subsidiaries will give PdVSA a 60% interest in an offshore block, as well as gas licenses, and a 25.2% non-operated interest in the Petroindependiente joint venture located in the western part of the country.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Summer Is 'Aggressive Time Frame' for Lower Gas Costs, Energy Secretary Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Jared Mitovich

WASHINGTON-Energy Secretary Chris Wright now considers summer to be an "aggressive time frame" for when oil and gasoline prices might decline.

Wright is the latest Trump administration official to issue a bullish outlook for the cost of gas, now averaging $4.13 a gallon nationwide, according to the AAA. President Trump conceded on Sunday that energy prices could be higher around the time U.S. voters head to polls for midterm elections this fall.

"The president knew going into this that if you disrupt the flow of energy in the short term, you're gonna push up energy prices," Wright said during an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit in Washington. "He was willing to say, I'm not going to kick that can down the road as other administrations have."

Oil prices could peak in the next few weeks, but won't decline meaningfully until ship traffic picks up through the Strait of Hormuz, Wright said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Rare Earths Americas Files for IPO
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Rare Earths Americas filed for an initial public offering.

The exploration-stage critical minerals company did not say how many shares it would list or at what price. It said it intends to apply to list shares on the New York Stock Exchange American under the symbol REA, according to a Monday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company's portfolio includes one project about 68 miles from Atlanta and two projects in Brazil, according to the filing. It said it is set up to be a non-Chinese supplier of rare earth minerals in alignment with Western industrial and national security priorities.

It intends to use proceeds from the offering to advance its three projects, prioritizing its Shiloh Project in Georgia.

The company reported a loss of $9.9 million, or 85 cents a share, in 2025.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Rare Earths Americas, Exploration-Stage Rare Earths Miner, Files for NYSE American IPO
data.description:
Rare Earths Americas, an exploration-stage developer of rare earth mineral projects in the U.S. and Brazil, filed to go public on the NYSE American under the symbol REA. The company plans an initial public offering led by Cantor and Stifel, with B. Riley Securities and Canaccord Genuity as additional underwriters. Proceeds will fund exploration and advancement of its Shiloh (Georgia), Alpha (Bahia, Brazil), and Constellation (Minas Gerais, Brazil) projects, with priority on Shiloh.

Business Description

Rare Earths Americas is an exploration-stage company advancing a portfolio of rare earth mineral projects aimed at supplying magnet-grade oxides, particularly the heavy rare earth elements dysprosium (Dy) and terbium (Tb). The company’s three material projects are Shiloh in Georgia (USA), which hosts monazite mineralization with high-grade early exploration results and benefits from private land and robust local infrastructure; the Alpha Project in Bahia, Brazil, an ionic adsorption clay (IAC) deposit with an inferred mineral resource of 201.7 Mt at 1,520 ppm TREO; and the Constellation Project in Minas Gerais, Brazil, an IAC deposit with an inferred mineral resource of 266.2 Mt at 2,637 ppm TREO.

The company is pre-revenue and focused on systematic exploration, resource definition, metallurgical testing, permitting, and engineering to de-risk its portfolio. With exposure to both the U.S. and Brazil, Rare Earths Americas aims to help diversify critical mineral supply chains away from China and align with Western industrial and national security initiatives. Near-term priorities include expanding drilling and metallurgical test work, advancing S‑K 1300 technical report workstreams, and pursuing partnerships and financing to accelerate development.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Global magnet rare earth oxide demand projected to grow strongly, with NdPr demand rising from ~110 kt in 2024 to ~350 kt by 2035.
  • Market growth: Adamas Intelligence forecasts MREO demand to grow at ~8.2–8.3% CAGR through 2035–2040; Dy and Tb demand ~7% annually through 2040.
  • Market position: Dual‑jurisdiction (U.S. and Brazil) explorer focused on heavy rare earths to support Western supply chains and national security priorities.
  • Key competitors: Chinese producers dominate mining and processing; Western suppliers include MP Materials and Lynas, with emerging Brazilian IAC projects.
  • Industry trends: Supply concentration in China/Myanmar, policy support in the U.S. and allied nations, rising demand from EVs, wind, robotics, and advanced motion technologies.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: No commercial sales to date; pre-revenue exploration stage.
  • Locations: Principal office in Manchester, Georgia; field offices in Georgia and Brazil.
  • Geographic presence: Dual-jurisdiction projects in Georgia (U.S.) and Bahia/Minas Gerais (Brazil).
  • Partnerships: Underwriting syndicate includes Cantor, Stifel, B. Riley Securities, and Canaccord Genuity; various option/lease agreements for mineral rights.
  • Other key metrics: Shiloh land position ~1,927 acres; Alpha inferred resource 201.7 Mt @ 1,520 ppm TREO; Constellation inferred resource 266.2 Mt @ 2,637 ppm TREO; Brazil tenement control at Homer ~1,233 km²; workforce includes 12 full-time employees, 1 part-time employee, and 8 consultants (as of March 31, 2026).

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): No revenue (exploration stage)
  • Operating income: Operating loss of $9.455 million (2025)
  • Net income: Net loss of $9.930 million (2025); net loss of $3.975 million (2024)

Management

  • Donald Swartz, Chief Executive Officer and President - Mining executive with prior leadership roles at American Rare Earths Limited and Westmoreland Coal; MBA (University of Denver), BS Mining Engineering (West Virginia University).
  • Jennifer Grafton, Chief Operating Officer, General Counsel and Secretary - Former EVP & General Counsel at E2open; prior senior roles at Westmoreland; director roles at Dakota Gold and Farmland Partners; JD (University of Denver), MBA (University of Michigan).
  • Cheryl Kerr, Chief Accounting Officer and Treasurer - Former Senior Director of Accounting at Lumen Technologies; Certified Public Accountant; BBA and MBA (University of Oklahoma).

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Rare Earths Americas, Inc.
  • Filing date: April 13, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: REA
  • Exchange: NYSE American
  • Lead underwriters: Cantor; Stifel; B. Riley Securities; Canaccord Genuity
  • Use of proceeds: Fund land acquisition and option payments, drilling, metallurgical test work, permitting and S-K 1300 technical reports at Shiloh (priority); exploration, evaluation, land consolidation, metallurgy, engineering and permitting at Alpha and Constellation; and remaining funds for non‑material projects (including Homer and Liberty Peak), working capital and general corporate purposes.

Original SEC Filing: Rare Earths Americas, Inc. - S-1 - Apr. 13, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Wheat futures advance on worries over dry US weather
data.description:
By Tom Polansek

U.S. wheat futures rose on worries about unfavorably dry weather on Monday, analysts said, as prices recovered after falling to a one-month low on Friday.

An expanding drought has fueled concerns about potential crop losses in the Plains region, the heart of the winter wheat belt, before farmers start harvesting their next crops in the next few months.

After trading ended, the U.S. Department of Agriculture said in a weekly report that 34% of the nation's winter wheat was in good or excellent condition as of Sunday. That was down from 35% a week earlier and 47% a year ago.

"All three classes of wheat had a strong day as many areas in the west missed weekend rains," Chad Pater, senior commodity broker at CHS Hedging, said in a note.

Chicago Board of Trade May soft red winter wheat (WK26) finished up 11-1/4 cents at $5.82-1/4 per bushel after sliding on Friday to $5.67-1/2, the contract's lowest level since March 4.

K.C. May hard red winter wheat (KWK26) ended up 12-1/2 cents at $6.03-1/4 a bushel and set its highest price since April 3.

CBOT May corn (CK26) settled 3/4 cent lower at $4.40-1/4 per bushel, while May soybeans (SK26) fell 13-1/2 cents to $11.62-1/4 per bushel after reaching $11.83-3/4, the contract's highest price since March 16.

U.S. farmers have planted 5% of the nation's corn crop and 6% of soybeans, ahead of the five-year averages, USDA said in its report.

In Ukraine, a major grain exporter, consultancy APK-Inform trimmed its forecasts for the country's 2026 wheat and corn harvests.

Commodity firm Argus also lowered its estimate for Ukraine's 2026 wheat crop, though the outlook remained the highest since Russia's invasion in 2022 and above the four-year average. The IKAR consultancy lowered its estimate for Russia's wheat exports in April.

Over the weekend, the Black Sea region received light showers, along with near-freezing-to-below-freezing temperatures in Ukraine, weather firm Vaisala said.

"Some damage to winter wheat was possible," the firm said.

Traders also monitored the Iran war, which has spiked prices for fertilizer needed to plant corn. President Donald Trump said the U.S. military began a blockade of ships leaving Iran's ports and oil prices climbed back over $100 per barrel.

data.title: First Horizon Bank Welcomes Rite Moisio as Director of Private Client Banking for the New Orleans Market
data.description:
NEW ORLEANS, April 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — First Horizon Bank ( or "First Horizon") is pleased to announce that Rite Moisio has joined the First Horizon team as Director of Private Client Banking for the New Orleans market. She brings extensive experience in private banking and wealth management, with a strong focus on serving high-net-worth individuals and families.

Prior to joining First Horizon, Moisio began her career in New Orleans serving as Vice President and Private Banker at JP Morgan Chase. In 2013, she transitioned to Hancock Whitney, where she held leadership roles within the Private Bank, most recently serving as Senior Vice President and Team Lead of the New Orleans Wealth Management group. Over the course of her career, she has developed deep client relationships across the Gulf South.

"Rite is a proven leader with a strong track record of serving clients and building high-performing teams," said Tony Adams, Gulf States Regional President for First Horizon. "Her expertise and passion for the New Orleans community align with our strategy as we continue to grow our Private Banking presence across this market."

In addition to her professional experience, Moisio is actively involved in the New Orleans community and has supported several nonprofit organizations focused on youth development, education and community well-being.

About First Horizon

First Horizon Corporation , with $83.9 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025, is a leading regional financial services company, dedicated to helping our clients, communities and associates unlock their full potential with capital and counsel. Headquartered in Memphis, TN, the banking subsidiary First Horizon Bank operates in 12 states concentrated in the southern U.S. The Company and its subsidiaries offer commercial, private banking, consumer, small business, wealth and trust management, retail brokerage, capital markets, fixed income, and mortgage banking services. First Horizon has been recognized as one of the nation's best employers by Fortune and Forbes magazines and a Top 10 Most Reputable U.S. Bank. More information is available at www.FirstHorizon.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/first-horizon-bank-welcomes-rite-moisio-as-director-of-private-client-banking-for-the-new-orleans-market-302740966.html

SOURCE First Horizon Bank

B2Gold executives will host a conference call to discuss the results on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at 8:00 am PT / 11:00 am ET.

About B2Gold

B2Gold is a responsible international gold producer headquartered in Vancouver, Canada. Founded in 2007, today, B2Gold has operating gold mines in Canada, Mali, Namibia and the Philippines, and numerous development and exploration projects in various countries.

data.title: Marvel Biosciences Announces an Amendment to the Conversion Price for Marvel's Proposed Convertible Debenture Offering
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - April 13, 2026) - Marvel Biosciences Corp. ("Marvel" or the "Company") Further to the Company's press release of April 8, 2026, announcing its intention to complete a non-brokered private placement offering of unsecured convertible debentures ("Debentures") in the principal amount of up to $500,000 (the "Offering"), Marvel wishes to announce that it has amended the conversation price for the Debentures, which are convertible at the holder's option into Common Shares, to a conversion price of $0.17 per Common Share. All other terms of the Debenture and of the Offering remain unchanged and the Offering remains subject to approval of the TSX Venture Exchange.

About Marvel Biosciences Corp.Marvel Biosciences Corp., and its wholly owned subsidiary, Marvel Biotechnology Inc., is a Calgary- based pre-clinical stage pharmaceutical development biotechnology company. The Company is developing MB 204, a novel fluorinated derivative of the approved anti-Parkinson's drug Istradefylline, the only clinically approved adenosine A2a antagonist. A significant and growing body of scientific evidence suggests drugs that block the adenosine A2a receptor, such as MB-204, could be useful in treating other neurological diseases such as autism, depression and Alzheimer's Disease. The Company is actively investigating its potential in addressing other neurodevelopmental disorders, such as Rett Syndrome and Fragile X Syndrome, to expand its therapeutic reach.

Contact Information:

Marvel Biosciences Corp.

J. Roderick (Rod) Matheson, Chief Executive Officer or

Dr. Mark Williams, President, and Chief Science Officer 

Tel: 403 770 2469

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as the term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Disclaimer

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains certain statements which constitute forward-looking statements or information ("forward-looking statements"), including statements regarding Marvel's business, the Offering, including the use of proceeds of the Private Placement. Such forward-looking statements are subject to numerous risks and uncertainties, some of which are beyond Marvel's control, including the impact of general economic conditions, the current share price of Marvel's common shares, TSX Venture acceptance and market acceptance of the Private Placement, industry conditions, currency fluctuations, the lack of availability of qualified personnel or management, stock market volatility and the ability to access sufficient capital from internal and external sources. Although Marvel believes that the expectations in its forward-looking statements are reasonable, they are based on factors and assumptions concerning future events which may prove to be inaccurate. Those factors and assumptions are based upon currently available information. Such statements are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that could influence actual results or events and cause actual results or events to differ materially from those stated, anticipated or implied in the forward-looking information. As such, readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on the forward-looking information, as no assurance can be provided as to future results, levels of activity or achievements. The forward-looking statements contained in this document are made as of the date of this document and, except as required by applicable law, Marvel does not undertake any obligation to publicly update or to revise any of the included forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise. The forward-looking statements contained in this document are expressly qualified by this cautionary statement.

data.title: Hims & Hers to Announce First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on May 11, 2026
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 13, 2026--

Hims & Hers Health, Inc. ("Hims & Hers", ), the leading health and wellness platform, today announced that it will report first quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on Monday, May 11, 2026. The company will host a live conference call to discuss the results at 5:00 p.m. ET the same day.

The conference call can be accessed by dialing (888) 510-2630 for U.S. participants and (646) 960-0137 for international participants, referencing conference ID 1704296. A live audio webcast will be available at https://investors.hims.com and will be archived for one year.

Upcoming Conference Participation

Hims & Hers also announced that members of the company's management team will be participating in the Evercore TMT Conference on June 2, 2026 in San Francisco.

About Hims & Hers Health, Inc.

Hims & Hers is the leading health and wellness platform on a mission to help the world feel great through the power of better health.

We believe how you feel in your body and mind transforms how you show up in life. That's why we're building a future where nothing stands in the way of harnessing this power. Hims & Hers normalizes health & wellness challenges--and innovates on their solutions--to make feeling happy and healthy easy to achieve. No two people are the same, so the Company provides access to personalized care designed for results.

For more information, please visit https://investors.hims.com/.
